mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strengthening consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically precedes broad-based equity rallies by compressing risk premiums and lifting forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The forward return differential between high and low HOUST deciles is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across all post-2005 observations.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which directly supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity earnings growth such that SPX forward returns turn negative during INDPRO acceleration phases for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.3% triggers inflation hedging demands and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPI spikes above the threshold, resulting in a positive forward return frequency exceeding 55% during triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and nonfarm payrolls expansion confirms a self-reinforcing economic upswing that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy, with a cumulative forward return spread below 1.5% over any 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment rises while the unemployment rate remains stubbornly above 7.5% signals structural labor market inefficiencies and wage-push inflation risks, which erode equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while UNRATE remains above 7.5%
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail and food service sales indicates weakening consumer demand, which directly forecasts lower corporate revenues and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as earnings revisions turn adverse.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite consecutive RSAFS declines, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 2-year out-of-sample test.
